comments: Richard Skinner sits in at short notice as Tommy is “stranded on a plane over Europe”

sessions.details: recorded 1971-05-10 for Bob Harris. First TX 1971-05-31.
recorded 1972-09-25 for John Peel (Session #2). First TX 1972-11-07.

track_listing.details: Into The Black; LP-Rust Never Sleeps

transcript.text: On 275 and 285 medium wave and stereo VHF, it's the Friday Rock Show.
Good evening, this is Richard Skinner sitting in for Tommy Vance, who's literally in the air right now, stranded on a plane over Europe.

But despite Tommy's absence, there's some great music on tonight's programme. We've got vintage sessions from Genesis, plus records from Kiss, Neil Young and Crazy Horse, Pink Floyd, Nick Lowe, Joni Mitchell, Hot Tuna and Queen.
Add to that the Friday Night Connection results and this week's new competition, and it sounds like a great two hours here on Radio 1.
Let's kick things off with Led Zeppelin.
Kashmir, Led Zeppelin from Physical Graffiti. A pretty good track, that.
And I, for one, can't wait to see Led Zeppelin when they step out live at Nebworth.
We played that track, by the way, for Helen Agnew, who hangs out in Rotherham.
Maureen Jones from Exeter Youth Hostel, and a lad called Cad from Shropshire.
He says he loves Alison, and I hope Alison loves Kiss, because they've got some dirty living to do.
And Chelsea Girl, that's their new single, just about to come out, and it comes from the LP Life in a Day.
Before that, you had a bit of Dirty Living, another new track, that one from Kiss.
It's 18 after 10, you're listening to the Friday Rock Show, I'm Richard Skinner, and it's time for our Vintage Session this week.
When a group's as popular as Genesis, it's not surprising that we've got a whole stack of requests for them.
In fact, we've dug up two sessions from Genesis, one from 1971, another from 1972.
First, a couple of tracks from a 1971 Sound of the Seventies session.
In a moment, Stagnation, a track you know from the album Trespass,
but first, the BBC version of a track from Nursery Crime, its musical box.
Genesis with Stagnation, and before that, Musical Box, two tracks from a 1971 BBC session.
By the way, the line-up on that recording,
Steve Hackett, Anthony Banks, Peter Gabriel, Mike Rutherford and Phil Collins' memories.
He first enjoyed those tracks, in fact, on Sounds of the Seventies back in May 1971.
And I know for certain that George Barker and Fleet, Dave Moss from Cardiff,
Stephen Brunt from Cambridgeshire and Jan from the Sandown High School on the Isle of Wight
are pretty happy now because they all requested Genesis Sessions.
For all of them, and for more people, we have more from Genesis later.
but now from their brand new LP

Friday Night Connection is set by this week's winner,
Brian Luder of Singapore, Burma Road, Romsey in Hampshire.
Congratulations, Brian. You've got a goody there.
If you think you spotted Brian's connection, well, this is what to do.
Send a postcard to the Friday Rock Show, Friday Night Connection,
BBC Radio 1, London W1A, 4WW.
That's the Friday Rock Show, Friday Night Connection, BBC Radio 1,
London W1A, 4WW.
And don't forget, you can't win the competition
unless you devise another Friday Night Connection.
So put your ideas on the card along with the answer,
and we look forward to hearing from you.
This is the Friday Night Rock Show with Richard Skinner
sitting in for Tommy Vance,
and Joni Mitchell has got a brand-new album out.
It's called Mingus, because on it,
Joni worked with the legendary jazz musician Charlie Mingus.
The sessions took place just before Charlie's death last year,
and his voice, as well as his music, appears on the album.
I've never had it too hard, you know.
All my life, you know, everything I touch turned to gold.
I'm not rich, but, you know,

that's new from Joni, and it's on Asylum Records.
And now it's Genesis time again,
as we play a session this time from September 1972.

for Keith Hargreaves in Olney in Buckinghamshire, who asked for Genesis.

and a couple of people in Harrow, Colin Spate and Simon Kenning.

Three tracks from a John Peel Genesis session from September 72
in order of appearance, Twilight Alehouse.
Get them out by Friday and Watcher of the Skies.
Now, something totally different.
If you've been listening to Andy Peebles this week,
you've been hearing a lot of Def Leppard.
Right now, we've got a track from their new EP.
It's called Get Your Rocks Off.
From their new double album, Live Killers.
And that was Tie Your Mother Down.
This has been the Friday Rock Show.
Hope you enjoy the music.
Aeroplanes permitting, you've got two chances
to catch up with Tommy Vance tomorrow
at 4 o'clock on Rock On
and again at 6.30 on In Concert,
when he'll be introducing the tourists and the police.
Looking ahead next week on the Friday Rock Show,
we've got archive sessions featuring The Faces with Rod Stewart.
Also, a brand-new session from The Ruts.
Join Tommy Vance next week.
In the meantime, from me, Richard Skinner,
it's goodnight and sleep tight.
